Class GenericProvider

java.lang.Object
dk.tij.jissuesystem.api.AbstractTokenProvider
dk.tij.jissuesystem.provider.generic.GenericProvider
All Implemented Interfaces:
IIssueProvider
public class GenericProvider extends AbstractTokenProvider implements IIssueProvider
A configurable IIssueProvider implementation for interacting with arbitrary issue tracking APIs.

The GenericProvider allows consumers to specify custom API endpoints and optionally provide lambda-based strategies for parsing labels and building issue payloads.

This provider is useful for interacting with services that expose REST APIs but do not have a dedicated provider implementation in this library.

Configuration

Instances should be created using the GenericProvider.Builder: 
GenericProvider provider = GenericProvider.builder()
    .owner("owner")
    .repo("repo")
    .token("api-token")
    .labelsEndpoint(URI.create("https://api.example.com/labels"))
    .issueEndpoint(URI.create("https://api.examples.com/issues"))
    .build();

Custom label parsing and payload generation logic may also be supplied:

GenericProvider provider = GenericProvider.builder()
    .owner("owner")
    .repo("repo")
    .token("api-token")
    .labelsEndpoint(URI.create("https://api.example.com/labels"))
    .issueEndpoint(URI.create("https://api.examples.com/issues"))
    .labelParser(raw -> ...)
    .payloadBuilder(issue -> ...)
    .build();

Requests are executed asynchronously using HttpClient

Since:
0.2.0

  • Constructor Details

    • GenericProvider

      protected GenericProvider(String owner, String repo, String token)
      Constructs a new GenericProvider with the specified repository context and authentication token.

      Initialises default label parser (GenericLabelParser) and payload builder (GenericPayloadBuilder).

      Parameters:
      owner - the repository owner, organisation, or namespace
      repo - the repository or project name
      token - the authentication token for API requests
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any of the parameters are null or blank

  • Method Details

    • configure

      public GenericProvider configure(URI labelsEndpoint, URI issueEndpoint)
      Configures the API endpoints used by this provider.

      This method is primarily intended for internal use by the GenericProvider.Builder. It initialises the endpoints required for retrieving labels and creating issues.

      Parameters:
      labelsEndpoint - the endpoint used to retrieve repository labels
      issueEndpoint - the endpoint used to create issues
      Returns:
      this provider instance
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either endpoint is null

    • fetchLabels

      public CompletableFuture<Set<Label>> fetchLabels()
      Retrieves the set of labels available for the configured repository.

      The request is executed asynchronously using HttpClient. The raw API response is passed to the configured label parser to produce a set of Label objects.

      Specified by:
      fetchLabels in interface IIssueProvider
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the parsed labels.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the provide has not been configured

    • report

      public CompletableFuture<HttpResponse<String>> report(Issue issue)
      Reports a new issue to the configured issue endpoint.

      The issue is converted into a request payload using the configured payload builder and submitted via an asynchronous HTTP POST request.

      Specified by:
      report in interface IIssueProvider
      Parameters:
      issue - the issue to report
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the HTTP response
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if issue is null
      IllegalStateException - if the provider has not been configured

    • builder

      public static GenericProvider.Builder builder()
      Creates a new GenericProvider.Builder used to construct a GenericProvider.
      Returns:
      a new GenericProvider.Builder instance
